pattern: Greedily pair each value with its double by processing numbers in order of absolute value, so the smallest-magnitude unmatched number is always paired with the only candidate that can be its partner.
hints: Each pair consists of a number and exactly twice that number, so think in terms of consuming the array two elements at a time using a count of how many of each value remain.
Process the values by increasing absolute value: the element with the smallest magnitude that is still unused can only ever be the smaller half of its pair.

For that smallest-magnitude value, you must have a matching count of its double available; if any value cannot be fully paired this way the reordering is impossible, and zeros must come in even counts.

edge_inputs.description: values present but cannot all be paired
two equal nonzero values cannot pair with each other
odd count of zeros leaves one unpaired
looks pairable greedily the wrong way

stress.description: construct genuinely-pairable arrays half the time by building from base values, otherwise random even-length arrays
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    half = max(1, min(size, 15000) // 2)
    if rng.random() < 0.5:
        arr = []
        for _ in range(half):
            base = rng.randint(-50, 50)
            arr.append(base)
            arr.append(2 * base)
        rng.shuffle(arr)
        return [arr]
    n = 2 * half
    arr = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [arr]
stress.note: the constructed half guarantees 'true' cases; random even-length arrays mostly give 'false'
